ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR All Remaining Matters From The Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee Including The Issue Of Venue -
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Continued To Mar-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Liza de Vries for Herb Thomas.
Daniel Conrad for Sharon Mays.

The Court stated that the case should stay in California, that it can not transfer venue to Texas and that issue is whether it should remain in San Francisco or Alameda. Ms. de Vries stated that she would be happy to transfer the case to Alameda or have a short continuance to allow discovery and perhaps get the matter resolved. Mr. Conrad stated that he has no problem with the case staying in San Francisco and that they have agreed as to sale of the Oakdale property. The remaining matters from the petition for appointment of Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to March 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Liza De Vries for Herb Thomas (Petitioner).
Elizabeth Hanowsky for Sharon Mays (Obejctor).

The Court stated that there is the issue of venue, who has the right to the position of Trustee, whether allocation was properly done, document collection, and whether Kenneth needs a Guardian Ad Litem and his status. Ms. De Vries stated that she has been meeting and conferring with Mr. Conrad's office, that they agree to and propose appointment of Mr. Thomas in the Bypass Trust and continuing the issue of the Survivor's Trust, that she has been working with the Golden Gate Regional Center ("GGRC"), that the GGRC is working with Kenneth regarding the Special Needs Trust ("SNT") and that Kenneth is non-verbal. Ms. De Vries also stated that it was the parents intention that who ever serves as Trustee also serve as Trustee for Kenneth's SNT, that Mr. Thomas is ready to serve and that she can bring a petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem. Ms. De Vries also stated that the parties agree to provide documents once Mr. Thomas is appointed and that the Oakdale property is ready to be sold. Mr. Hanowsky stated that they do not object to Mr. Thomas being appointed in the Bypass Trust, that her Client is the acting Trustee in the Survivor's Trust and is ready to share information, that they did not object to venue, that they acknowledge that the property is here and that the Trustee has been in Texas since she began acting as Trustee. Ms. De Vries stated that Mr. Thomas was nominated by Catherine, that his Attorney is here and that appointment of Ms. Mays is improper. The Court advised Counsel that location of the property and the Attorney is not a basis for venue, that it will approve appointment of Mr. Thomas in the Bypass Trust, that all of the other issues including venue are continued December 5th and that by signing the order today it does not intend that the Court recognizes the issue of venue. Ms. De Vries stated that the Bypass has no assets. Ms. Hanowsky clarified that the Bypass has two empty lots in Lake County and one empty lot in Richmond. The Court will sign the order appointing Herb Thomas as Trustee of the Bypass Trust. Bond is not imposed with this order. The burden is on Mr. Thomas to get a bond immediately when there are assets. The balance of the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee is continued to December 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
